key_insight: NOTES combines a neural operator with CMA-ES to optimize PDE-constrained designs in a much smaller latent space while preserving topology-aware structure and transferability.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to fast, robust inverse design for solid mechanics and other physics systems where direct optimization in the full design space is too expensive.
method: Train a DeepONet-based neural operator to learn topology-aware latent representations of designs.
Run CMA-ES in the compact latent space instead of the original high-dimensional parameterization.
Decouple topology learning from the PDE solver to support transfer to unseen operating conditions.
evidence_signal: In nanophotonic beam deflector design, the method compresses the design space from 256 to 25 variables and achieves over 95% efficiency, while in structural optimization it reaches compliance down to 246.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the learned latent representation, so training data coverage and transfer across new physics settings may still limit robustness.
paper_title: Neural Operator-enabled Topology-informed Evolutionary Strategy for PDE-Constrained Optimization
